items.answer: Yes, your policy may pay for a rental car or other transportation expenses up to $900 per day, depending on the type of loss and coverage provided.

items.answer: Most standard policies will cover a friend who has borrowed your car; however, if a friend borrows your car on a regular basis, the insurance company most likely will not pay for the loss.

items.answer: An uninsured motor vehicle is a land motor vehicle or trailer of any type that either has no bodily injury liability bond or policy at the time of the accident, or has a bodily injury liability bond or policy but its limit for bodily injury liability is less than the minimum limit specified by the financial responsibility law of the state where your covered auto is principally garaged. It also includes a hit-and-run vehicle whose operator or owner cannot be identified and hits you or any family member, a vehicle you own that is not insured for this coverage under this policy, or a vehicle to which a bodily injury liability bond or policy applies but the bonding or insuring company denies coverage or becomes insolvent.

items.answer: An insurance score is a number used by insurance companies to predict the likelihood that an applicant or policyholder will file claims. Companies use it because studies show a correlation between this score and the likelihood of filing a claim [2].

items.answer: A Good Driver is a person who has been licensed for at least three consecutive years and has no more than one-point on their driving record. They must receive rates that are at least 20% lower than a non-Good Driver's rates would be at the same insurance company.
